title: Warhammer Quest (1995) - Still the Gold Standard - Part 2 - The Official Expansions
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DY9enl751Hk

Daniel (@dungeondive) surveys every official Warhammer Quest expansion, starting with the warrior packs. Each adds a hero with unique settlement interactions, skills, and levelling paths: the Pit Fighter (brutal melee), Chaos Warrior (random chaos attributes each game), Warrior Priest (healing that might hurt you), Witch Hunter (Daniel's prototypical Old World character), Imperial Noble (access to the exclusive Luigi and Salvatore shop), Elf Ranger (ranger or mage build), War Dancer (battlefield mobility), Troll Slayer, and the elusive Bretonnian Knight. He notes the absence of female characters and praises modern games for improving representation.

The three Treasure Card packs (54 cards each) dramatically expand loot variety. The two big-box expansions—Catacombs of Terror (undead-themed, multi-level dungeons, Daniel's favourite) and Lair of the Orc Lord (orc-themed, lower-level adventures)—each add quest books, bestiaries, GM-led multi-level campaigns, tiles, and miniatures, though Daniel finds them stingy on cards. The ultra-rare Pits and Perils expansion (rotating bridge, special rooms) may be the rarest board game expansion he's encountered. Again: don't pay collector prices—Little Monk's rebalanced versions are available free through the Facebook fan community.

----

Which warrior expansion would you add to your party first—Chaos Warrior, Witch Hunter, or Pit Fighter?
